A1.guidance: yes/no or either/or questions using only known vocabulary. Accept single-word answers. Purpose: reduce anxiety. Example: Te gusta mas el cafe o el te? Keep to 1-2 teacher turns.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Listening comprehension tasks require audio resources not suited to WarmUp

levelSpecificNotes.note: Keep to 1-2 scripted exchanges only; model the turn before asking student to respond
Use only yes/no question format (Quién es quién) to keep language minimal

A2.guidance: Open personal questions using present tense. Expect a 2-3 sentence response. Example: Que haces los fines de semana? Include 2-3 suggested follow-up questions. Note: Use preterite only if the student has already learned it.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Listening comprehension tasks require audio resources not suited to WarmUp

levelSpecificNotes.note: Limit to 2-3 teacher turns maximum; do not extend into full discussion
Use only simple concrete vocabulary categories (animals, food, objects)

B1.guidance: Opinion prompts with light justification. Que opinas de...? Por que? Tied to the lesson topic. 2-3 follow-up questions. No scaffolding phrases needed.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Listening comprehension tasks require audio resources not suited to WarmUp

levelSpecificNotes.note: Keep social simulations short (1-2 minutes); this is warm-up, not full practice

B2.guidance: News-related or cultural discussion starters. Expects 4-5 sentence connected discourse. agree/disagree or headline prediction format. 2-3 follow-up questions.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Listening comprehension tasks require audio resources not suited to WarmUp

levelSpecificNotes.note: Intercultural comparisons work well as opinion hooks at B2; student leads the comparison

C1.guidance: Provocative statement, abstract prompt, or ethical dilemma. Expects nuanced argumentation. Single trigger, no follow-ups needed; teacher improvises. circumlocution or semantic mapping as alternatives.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Listening comprehension tasks require audio resources not suited to WarmUp

levelSpecificNotes.note: Negotiation/problem-solving works as a warm-up only if the scenario is very brief (1-2 turns to establish positions)

C2.guidance: Devil advocate positions or mediation warm-ups (summarize a concept for a non-expert). Expects register-aware extended discourse. Same trigger types as C1.

forbiddenExerciseTypes.reason: Listening comprehension tasks require audio resources not suited to WarmUp

levelSpecificNotes.note: Oral mediation as warm-up: ask student to explain a concept from last session in simple terms for a hypothetical non-expert audience
